top_signals.title: Philippines Seeks Long-Term Oil Supply Deal With Russia
top_signals.summary: The Philippines is looking to formalize a deal to buy oil from Russia on a more permanent basis amid the energy crisis and heightened geopolitical tensions, Philippines President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. has said. The Philippines has been one of the worst-hit Asian economies in the current oil supply crisis triggered by the Middle East conflict, and has turned to Russia for ad-hoc spot supply to cover the shortfall of cargoes from the Middle East. Now the Asian country is looking to make the Russian oil arrangement more permanent as a means to diversify…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Indian Billionaires Set for Jackpot on National Stock Exchange IPO
top_signals.summary: Some of India’s wealthiest investors stand to get even richer from their shareholdings in the nation’s stock exchange, which filed for an initial public offering this week.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: King’s Cross shows the way to solve London’s worspace shortage
top_signals.summary: Businesses must partner with developers to build the office space London needs, says Matt Flood We’ve all seen the headlines – demand for office space in the City of London and the West End only seems to be growing.
